body.title: pe_chain HYPOTHESIZE: LLM stuck on paraphrase across retries — verbatim feedback ignored
body.result: Superseded by routing diagnosis: the 'stickiness' was 7B-model behavior; cert debugger silently routed to local Ollama qwen2.5:7b instead of cloud qwen-2.5-coder-32b. With 32B routing (Igor's autonomous default), HYPOTHESIZE produces verbatim-valid edits cleanly (validated cert walk-02 substitute attempt 8 → commit b092865d). Test-rig fix tracked in T-cert-debugger-env-mirror. The 5 candidate interventions documented here remain reusable if future 32B failures emerge.
body.description:
Cert walk-02 substitute attempt 4 (T-cc-queue-claim-honors-worker, 2026-04-30 18:48 UTC) exercised the new validate-then-retry path (T-pe-chain-hypothesize-retry). Both retries produced the SAME paraphrased old_string `if t['status'] == 'pending':` (single quotes, ==) even though actual code is `if t["status"] != "pending":` (double quotes, !=) and the retry prompt explicitly said: 'copy old_string EXACTLY as it appears — same quote style, same operators'.

Dump preserved: ~/.TheIgors/local/logs/cert_walk_02_substitute_attempt_4_retries_exhausted.json

The retry pattern is sound (Aider/Cursor use it). But this specific LLM (qwen-2.5-coder-32b via OpenRouter, temp 0.2) appears to have token-level stickiness: once it commits to a paraphrase in attempt 1, retries don't break the pattern. Possible interventions (NONE attempted yet — held per advisor stop rule):

1. Higher temperature on retry (0.2 → 0.7) to break the local minimum
2. Switch model on retry (e.g., escalate to a stronger tier on validation failure)
3. Pre-extract the line numbers from `actual` and ask LLM to specify line+target instead of copy-old_string
4. Auto-correct: when LLM's paraphrase resembles a real line in `actual`, substitute the real line transparently and proceed
5. Replace old_string-based diff with structured edits (line numbers, AST-based, or character offsets)

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/tools/pe_chain.py (pe_hypothesize + helpers)
**Design rules:** test-plan-or-why-not
**Scope boundary:** IN — investigating + picking ONE intervention from the list above. OUT — switching to a different LLM provider; rewriting the basket schema.
**Test plan:** integration test = re-run cert walk on T-cc-queue-claim-honors-worker (which now has documented failure-mode data). Success criteria: HYPOTHESIZE produces a verbatim-valid old_string matching one of consult.py / cc_queue.py / similar real codebase patterns.

Logged in cert dataset (~/.TheIgors/local/logs/cert_walk_02_substitute_attempt_4_retries_exhausted.json) for engram-repair training.
